questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are the manager of the department. You get to know that one of the subordinates is having a problem with his family, since his father is supposed to undergo bypass surgery but at the same time the subordinate is very important for the current project which you have undertaken. The subordinate wants two weeks' leave. What would you do?

choices.a: Give him your support by assuring him that his duty towards his father is more important
choices.b: Not empathise with the employee's situation and ask him to stay
choices.c: Get an extension for the project to be submitted as the employee is very efficient and you cannot trust anyone else
choices.d: Transfer the work to some other employee of similar caliber

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You have to accomplish a very important task for your headquarters within the next two days. Suddenly you meet with an accident. Your officer insists that you complete the task. You would [UPSC (CSAT) 2011]

choices.a: ask for an extension of deadline
choices.b: inform headquarters of your inability to finish on time
choices.c: suggest alternate person to Headquarters who may do the needful

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are passing by a river and you know swimming. Suddenly you hear the cry of a drowning child. You would

choices.a: wait to see, if some other person is there to help
choices.b: dive into the river to save him

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You have been asked to give an explanation for not attending an important official meeting. Your immediate boss who has not informed you about the meeting is now putting pressure on you not to place an allegation against him/her. You would [UPSC (CSAT) 2013]

choices.a: send a written reply explaining the fact
choices.b: seek an appointment with the top boss to explain the situation
choices.c: admit your fault to save the situation
choices.d: put the responsibility on the coordinator of the meeting for not informing

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are a team leader and you are supposed to hold a convention on HR issues but your team members are unable to get adequate sponsorship.

choices.a: You put in your money and hold the event as scheduled
choices.b: You try and motivate them that they can do it
choices.c: You postpone the event and give them some more time
choices.d: You tell them things can work out like this and cancel the event

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are a manager of a company and an employee does not turn up for work because his son was ill. You will [Hotel Mgmt 2004]

choices.a: tell him to come on time now onwards no matter what
choices.b: ask him how his son is and give him a day off
choices.d: ask how his son is and tell him to call the office, if even in future he decides not to come

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Your friend has lost his/her purse with your important documents in it. You would

choices.a: feel angry but do not react as anyone can make mistakes
choices.b: feel angry and ask him/her to replace/duplicate the documents
choices.c: understand the situation and tell him/her that it's ok and not to worry about it
choices.d: blame him/her for being careless and stop talking to him/her

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: After your graduation, you are offered a well paid Government job. However, your friend says that you have to pay bribe to get the appointment order. You [Hotel Mgmt 2004]

choices.a: go to some influential politician who can help
choices.b: accept the job by paying the bribe, consoling yourself that this is the present social setup
choices.c: accept the job by paying the bribe but firmly resolve that this is the last time you will pay bribe

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are alone in the house and there is quite a danger of thieves around. Just then, you hear a knock at the door. You would

choices.a: open the door to see who is there
choices.b: first peep out from the window to confirm whether you know the person
choices.d: ask the servant to see who is there

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: While travelling in your car, certain persons stop you on the way forcing you to take an injured child to the hospital. You would

choices.a: ask them to leave your way and then drive away
choices.b: ask them to first call the police
choices.c: immediately take the child to hospital
choices.d: get out of the car and ask some other person to help them

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are handling a time bound project. During the project review meeting, you find that the project is likely to get delayed due to lack of cooperation of the team members. You would [UPSC (CSAT) 2012]

choices.a: warn the team members for their non-cooperation
choices.c: ask for the replacement of team members
choices.d: ask for extension of time citing reasons
